name: Brin
greeting: A wiry woman with ink-stained fingers and shrewd eyes looks up from a ledger. 'Welcome to the Copper Mug. Sit down — I hear things in this business, and right now I'm hearing quite a lot.' She leans forward. 'Troubles to the south. Worse things than goblins, they say.'

dialogue.response: 'Three raids in a week on the forest road. Merchants won't travel after dark. The Guild's posted contracts — if you want coin and have a strong arm, talk to Hendricks at the Guild Hall. East of the square.'

'Obsidian Dungeon, they call it. Old place, volcanic rock. A guard went in last month — didn't come back. Captain Bren is fit to snap someone's head off about it. His post is at the Guard House, north of the Guild Hall.'

'Velian ruins, east past the dungeon. Ancient empire, long dead. Smart people don't go near them. Less-smart people go near them and come back with stories of something cold and luminous following them out. Something wraith-like.' She shudders.
'For sleep? The Inn's your best bet — west from the market. Morel runs a clean house, fair prices. Five gold for a room, warm bed and a proper breakfast. We just serve drinks here.'
'Good town, mostly. River floods once a decade and the Mayor panics, but that's just life here. The real problem is what's been stirring in the forest lately. I won't pretend it's nothing.'
